description:
Search within a Unity scene or prefab hierarchy and return precise object paths for later unity_yaml_read calls.

For .unity/.prefab files, live Unity Editor state is read first (unsaved changes visible), falling back to direct text YAML parsing when the Editor is unavailable. Use this when you know a name, component, tag/layer clue, prefab source, serialized field clue, or subtree but need the exact object path. Each result row shows full path, component names, active/static state, Tag, and Layer; prefab-source and serialized-field matches participate in matching but are omitted from rows. When sibling names repeat, paths use a 1-based segment ordinal such as `Root/Enemy[2]`.

When both query and component_filter are given, a result must satisfy both. Field matching (field_name/field_value in match_fields) is expensive on large scenes — combine it with path_prefix when possible.

file_path.description: Path to the Unity .unity or .prefab YAML file, relative to the project root (e.g. 'Assets/Scenes/Main.unity') or absolute path.

query.description: Optional text search over object paths, names, component names, annotations, and resolved prefab source paths. Case-insensitive substring by default; use 're:<pattern>' for a case-insensitive regular expression.

component_filter.description: Optional component filter. Use a component name such as 'Light' or 'Canvas', or comma-separated component names for OR matching.

match_fields.description: Optional comma-separated query scope. Supported values: path, name, component, annotation, tag, layer, prefab_source, field_name, field_value, default, all. Omit to use default matching. Use field_name or field_value only when you need serialized component field matching.

path_prefix.description: Optional hierarchy path prefix for searching one subtree, such as 'Environment/Lighting' or a returned duplicate-safe path like 'Root/Enemy[2]'.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of search results to print. Defaults to 50.
